leadText: Grovyle (Japanese: ジュプトル Juptile) is a Grass-type Pokémon introduced in Generation III. It evolves from Treecko starting at level 16 and evolves into Sceptile starting at level 36.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Grovyle is a bipedal Pokémon similar in appearance to a theropod dinosaur. It is primarily green with a red underside. There is a belt-like green stripe across its belly. Grovyle's hands have two clawed fingers and powerful legs with birdlike feet. Grovyle has three long leaves on its wrists, a large leaf on top of its head, and two tails that have a leafy appearance. This leafy appearance allows it to be camouflaged in the forests and dense jungles where it lives. Grovyle is said to be master at climbing trees in jungles. Grovyle's well-developed muscles make it an expert climber and allow it to leap quickly from branch to branch. The muscles are said to provide explosive bursts of power, which contribute to its incredible leaping capabilities. No Pokémon has been able to catch a fleeing Grovyle, no matter how fast they try to be. As mentioned in Sleep Style Dex , veteran researchers have difficulty finding the slumbering Grovyle due to its excellent camouflage. Grovyle has four different sleep styles: Knee-Up Sleep , Dream-Stance Sleep , Leaf-Tucked Sleep , and Atop-Belly Sleep . In Generation III , Leaf Blade was its signature move . Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Grovyle evolves from Treecko and evolves into Sceptile . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Treecko Grass → First Evolution Grovyle Grass → Second Evolution Sceptile Grass

Pokémon Mystery Dungeon: Explorers of Time, Darkness and Sky : Grovyle is a major character in the main storyline. He travels to the present to prevent the collapse of Temporal Tower by stealing Time Gears, actions that make him appear villainous for most of the game. The player was his partner in the future. He also stars in the Special Episode In the Future of Darkness . Pokémon Super Mystery Dungeon : The same Grovyle from Explorers of Time, Darkness, and Sky is recruitable via the Connection Orb. He requests assistance in rescuing Celebi . Spoilers end here. Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] This Pokémon was unavailable prior to Generation III. Generation III Hoenn #002 Kanto #— Ruby The leaves growing out of Grovyle 's body are convenient for camouflaging it from enemies in the forest. This Pok é mon is a master at climbing trees in jungles. Sapphire This Pok é mon adeptly flies from branch to branch in trees. In a forest, no Pok é mon can ever hope to catch a fleeing Grovyle however fast they may be. Emerald Leaves grow out of this Pok é mon 's body. They help obscure a Grovyle from the eyes of its enemies while it is in a thickly overgrown forest. FireRed Its strongly developed thigh muscles give it astounding agility and jumping performance. LeafGreen Generation IV Sinnoh #— Johto #— Diamond It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch. Pearl Platinum HeartGold It leaps from tree branch to tree branch quite swiftly. It shows astounding agility. SoulSilver Generation V Unova #— Black It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch. White Black 2 It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch. White 2 Generation VI Central Kalos #— Coastal Kalos #— Mountain Kalos #— Hoenn #002 X Its strongly developed thigh muscles give it astounding agility and jumping performance. Y It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch. Alpha Sapphire This Pokémon adeptly flies from branch to branch in trees. In a forest, no Pokémon can ever hope to catch a fleeing Grovyle however fast they may be. Generation VIII Galar #— Isle of Armor #— Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Sword, Shield , and Legends: Arceus . Brilliant Diamond It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch. Shining Pearl Generation IX Paldea #— Kitakami #— Blueberry #183 Lumiose #— Hyperspace #040 Scarlet Its strongly developed thigh muscles give it astounding agility and jumping performance. Violet Grovyle leaps from branch to branch in large trees, moving with ease. It propels itself with astounding speed and force. Legends: Z-A Its highly developed thigh muscles provide explosive bursts of power and incredible leaping capabilities. Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry AG041 Grovyle Ash's Pokédex Grovyle, the Wood Gecko Pokémon. Grovyle is the evolved form of Treecko and live deep within forests. Known as tree climbers, they jump easily from one tree to the next. AG066 Grovyle Ash's Pokédex Grovyle, the Wood Gecko Pokémon. Grovyle is well known for its dexterity in treetops, as it is often seen jumping from branch to branch. Even the fastest of Pokémon cannot catch a Grovyle in wooded areas. Episode Pokémon Source Entry XY075 Grovyle Serena's Pokédex Grovyle, the Wood Gecko Pokémon and an evolved form of Treecko. Grovyle has powerful legs, which allow it to move quickly and jump with great power.
